calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randall Schmidt for (former administrator) Andrew Wong. Steven Payette & Byron Done for (successor co-administrators) Kristina Suey & Eric Suey.

The Court stated that the parties have reached an agreement with the exception of fees to the administrator, that the pending petition alleges an agreement between Mr. Payette and former counsel to split fees, that the court has not seen the stipulation and that no objections have been filed. Mr. Done stated that Mr. Perry appeared on November 3, 2015 and stated his agreement. The Court stated that both sides have filed several pleadings regarding the remaining issues and asked if they were ready to submit. Mr. Schmidt stated that he would submit on the papers. Mr. Done stated that the former administrator filed a reply, cited section 12205 and that he does not take issue with the elements of delay but that the delay did cause harm to the estate, that the fact that work was done in the estate does not apply to 12205, that in regards to the statutory construction regarding the amount accounted for by the former administrator regarding the collection of rents that there is nothing to prohibit that amount being accounted for as he did collect them. The Court stated that it can take into account who did the work in the estate. Mr. Done stated that 12205 does not apply. The Court replied that 12205 does not control the entire decision. Mr. Schmidt stated that he addressed everything in his pleadings and asked if the order indicates that anything due to his client should go into the Special Needs Trust and that Kevin Kwok Yuen Leung is the trustee. Mr. Payette stated that according to the deposition Kevin is deceased. The Court told the parties to talk to each other and that if it is appropriate to submit a revised order and that if no changes are necessary to let us know. The accounting petition and the petition for approval of statutory fees were taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Approval Of Statutory Fees
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Approval Of Statutory Fees
Off Calendar Jan-05-2016 Continued To Jan-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Continued To Jan-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-03-2015 Continued To Dec-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for (co-administrators) Christina & Eric Suey. Byron Done, specially appearing with Mr. Payette. Randy Schmidt for the former administrator. James Perry also present.

The Court stated that there is an issue regarding statutory fees and the request for an equal split. Mr. Payette stated that Mr. Perry is here, and Mr. Perry confirmed his approval. Mr. Schmidt stated that he has an objection to file to the statutory fees, that he had attempted to file it but it was rejected as he needs to file a substitution of attorney first, that he had further filing issues as mandatory e-filing started yesterday but will get the documents filed today, that there was a special needs appointment of Kevin Leung in San Mateo County and that distribution in this matter will fund the trust. Mr. Payette stated that he has received the objections, that it is important to get the estate closed, that there are 3 blocked accounts, that they object to the objections and may need to litigate. Mr. Payette stated that are holding $50,000.00 in reserve which is substantial, that Mr. Schmidt is asking for $7,000.00, that he wants a further division of the statutory fees, that they are expecting a huge gain, explained that the date of death appraisal for the property was $220,000.00 but the property was sold for over $1,000,000.00 and explained the division of those funds. The Court asked if the reserve was more than necessary. Mr. Payette stated that income tax returns had not been filed back to 1999, that some have since been filed and that they may be audited. Mr. Schmidt stated that there was a property tax issue from a reassessment trigger, that the notice of such was sent to the wrong address and not paid and that he believes the reserve is sufficient. The Court clarified that it is the administrators fees which are in question and that the current petition seeks to pay the entirety to the successor co-administrators and that no one is contesting. Mr. Schmidt stated that he also objects to the amount of statutory fees, that there is an issue with the receipts and clarified that this was a report not an accounting. Mr. Done stated that they had not discussed that issue and that they could probably resolve the issue before the end of todays hearings. The accounting petition was continued to December 8, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Continued To Nov-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-15-2015 Continued To Oct-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette & Byron Done for Christina & Eric Suey. Randall Schmidt for Andrew Wong.

Mr. Payette confirmed that a settlement has been reached. Mr. Done stated that they have not had cooperation from the former administrator, that they are unable to get him to sign and do not know what else to do. Mr. Schmidt stated that they have resolved most issues, that they need one clarification and then will put the issue of statutory fees before the court and that they will be establishing a Special Needs Trust to receive distribution. Mr. Payette stated that he needs an address for Mr. Wong. Mr. Schmidt agreed to provide the address, explained that Mr. Wong had emergency surgery which has been their delay and that there is no issue with his signing the settlement agreement. The accounting petition was continued to October 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the amended petition is to be on file prior to that date.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for Eric Suey and Christina Suey. Randy Schmidt for Andrew Wong (Former Administrator).

Mr. Payette stated that they have not closed escrow, that they have an issue with Andrew not being able to vacate the premises, that they had an agreement that Andrew would vacate by October 31, 2014, that he is now leaving on November 15, 2014 and that the buyers are not on board. Mr. Schmidt explained that his Client has a medical condition and is unable to move, and that his Client had a place lined up but that it fell through. The Court noted that the funds may need to remain in escrow until the Special Needs Trust is established. Mr. Payette also stated that proof of service for the amended petition was attached to the petition when is was filed. The Court stated that it did not see a waiver of notice or an amended notice. Mr. Payette informed the Court that the Buyers were present. The Court noted all of the typographical errors in the petition. Mr. Payette stated that he filed an amended petition. 100% percent of the property was sold. 50% of the property is owned by this Estate and the other 50% is owned by an Estate in San Mateo. The sale was confirmed at $1,658,000.00 for 100% of the property based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt and review of the order). The proceeds of the sale are to be deposited into a blocked account in lieu of bond. (1)Petition For Removal Of Administrator For Cause; (2) To Appoint Petitioners As Co-Adminstrators; And (3) For An Accouting And Damages
Off Calendar Jun-25-2013 Continued To Oct-01-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for and with Eric Suey (Petitioner). James Perry for and with Kristina Suey (Petitioner). Randy Schmidt for and with Andrew Wong (Respondent). Norman Lew specially appearing for and with Andrew Wong.

The Parties were asked to step out of the courtroom to discuss how they plan to proceed or whether they can resolve the matter. Upon returning, Mr. Lew stated that they agreed to and will stipulate to Joe Chan (Former Associate of Mr. Lew) to receive as earned fees the amount of $231.50 in lieu of any statutory fees for services renderred. Mr. Perry stated that there is pending discovery with an extension to July 31, 2013, that all Parties agreed to meet at the property on July 31, 2013 for an inspection (photographs will be allowed), that the Tenants will be advised, that mediation will be global and that rents in the amount of $1,475.00 a month will be put into an Estate account. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation (order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation was signed in open Court). Mediation is to be completed by September 20, 2013. The Parties are to report to the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 20, 2013. The Petition for Removal of Administrator for Cause, etc. was continued to October 1, 2013 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204 for status on mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Judith DeAlba, CSR#5709 (650)-571-1473. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
